data.title: Methylphenidate Treatment of Children and Adolescents Diagnosed With ADHD and Its Influence on Comorbid Trichotillomania
data.brief_summary:
This study will evaluate the safety and effectiveness of methylphenidate in treating attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) in children with both ADHD and trichotillomania.

Trichotillomania is an impulse control disorder. There is growing evidences of the involvement of dopaminergic neurotransmission in the pathophysiology of trichotillomania. Reported increase in the prevalence of ADHD among patients with impulse control disorders, such as pathological gambling as well as trichotillom
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* DSM-IV Diagnosis of trichotillomania
* DSM-IV diagnosis of ADHD
* Has not taken stimulants or alpha-adrenergic medications for more than 2 weeks prior to entering the study.

Exclusion Criteria:

endpoints: Clinicial administered Massachusetts General Hospital Hair Pulling Scale for trichotillomania
